title: Sitnasuak CARES ACT 2020 Direct Assistance Program
summary: Shareholders impacted by COVID-19 can apply for up to $2,500. The deadline to apply is November 5, 2021. Funds distributed by November 19, 2021. See more information and apply. The post Sitnasuak CARES ACT 2020 Direct Assistance Program appeared first on Sitnasuak Native Corporation .

title: Education Spotlight: Angelica Dawn Alivrun Telfair
summary: BSNC descendant Angelica Dawn Alivrun Telfair is a student dedicated to her career goal of working as a counselor for youth struggling with addiction. he is currently working toward earning a dual master’s degree in Addiction Studies and Social Work at Eastern Washington University and plans to return to Alaska to work as a licensed substance use disorder pro...

title: Career Pathways Series-Branch Chief at BIA: Glenn Ivanoff
summary: BSNC’s Career Pathways Series helps showcase jobs held by successful shareholders and descendants. This series aims to raise awareness of these career options and the essential skills needed to succeed in these positions. title: Seward Peninsula Caribou Herd
summary: As fall comes to the tundra and waters of the Bering Strait region, many people are working to put away the fish and game that will help sustain them through the winter months. This is the pattern of life that has sustained our communities for millennia. One of the animals people will be harvesting...

summary: Recent advances in technology and the increasing concern over climate change have made renewable power generation a prime concern for electrical cooperatives everywhere. title: A Message from the President & CEO
summary: Fall 2021 Dear Shareholders, I hope you all had a safe and productive summer season. I always enjoy seeing photos posted by our people involving summer subsistence activities, including dried fish, jarred fish, ungimak (half-dried fish) and smoked strips. Many gallons of berries have also been gathered, which will be a great treat this winter for jams, pies, agutak, and even served frozen with...

title: Navy monitors marine mammals in GOA
summary: The U.S. Navy continues to be a world leader in marine species research and monitoring, having funded marine research programs, surveys, and data collection efforts since 1992, and since 2009 in the Gulf of Alaska.
